'''Avenger Squadron''' was an elite [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Starfleet]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] unit led by [[E.C. Nerwal|E.C. "Easy" Nerwal]]. They were assigned to [[Task Force Vengeance|Task Force ''Vengeance'']] on its quest to [[Spreading the Rebellion|pacify]] the [[Airam sector/Legends|Airam sector]].

Unlike regular Imperial starfighter squadrons, Avenger Squadron was permitted to modify their TIE craft with advanced and expensive gadgets previously deployed only by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]]'s experimental test units. This extra equipment included [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]], beam weapons—including jamming, tractor and decoy beams—and extra missile racks for increased warhead payload. However, except for the shields, those upgrades were only made available if and when the mission at hand called for a specific upgrade.

Initially, the standard starfighters employed by Avenger Squadron were modified [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]] with deflector shields, but other craft were also available to them.

[[File:Avenger_squadron_attack_Calenz.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Avenger Squadron attacks the ''[[Calenz]]''.]]
Veterans of Avenger Squadron flew TIE Interceptors that were marked with double white vertical stripes on both lower wing panels, including the squadron commander's Interceptor—designated ''Avenger Leader''—before receiving a [[TIE/ad starfighter]]—designated ''Avenger One''—to lead the squadron with.

During the campaign into the Airam sector, the whole squadron was outfitted with the advanced TIE/ad—which came to be as TIE Avengers—previously only flown by their commander. Subsequently, Avenger Squadron usually exclusively flew the TIE/ad starfighters.

During her attempt to turn [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], [[Ysanne Isard]] offered Horn command of the unit. Appealing to his vanity, Isard proposed that Horn could lead the elite Imperial pilots against [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], but he was unswayed.

==Behind the scenes==
The squadron was introduced with the ''Balance of Power'' expansion pack for the ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter]]'' game. When playing the Imperial campaign, the upgraded Interceptors with shields and later the TIE Avengers provided the player with starfighters similarly powerful to Rebel Alliance craft, unlike regular TIE Fighters which possessed no shields and were easily destroyed by a few hits. This is similar to the various upgraded special craft which are made available to the player in the [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] game.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter: Balance of Power Campaigns]]'' {{1st}}
